source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ACB = 90^\circ$, $AC = BC$, $BE \perp CE$ at point $E$, and $AD \perp CE$ at point $D$. Given $DE = 5$ and $AD = 9$, what is the length of $BE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution}: We have $\angle ACB = 90^\circ$ and $BE \perp CE$,\\
therefore, $\angle BCE + \angle ACD = 90^\circ$ and $\angle BCE + \angle CBE = 90^\circ$,\\
thus, $\angle ACD = \angle CBE$,\\
in $\triangle BEC$ and $\triangle CDA$,\\
\[
\left\{ \begin{array}{l}
\angle BEC = \angle CDA \\
\angle CBE = \angle ACD \\
BC = AC
\end{array} \right.,
\]
therefore, $\triangle ACD \cong \triangle CBE$ (by AAS),\\
thus, EC = AD = 9, and BE = DC,\\
since DE = 5,\\
thus, CD = EC - DE = 4,\\
therefore, BE = \boxed{4}.
